global_objective: Make this Claude Code plugin marketplace installable and legible on Claude, Codex, and Copilot by adding cross-platform packaging (generated Codex/Copilot per-plugin manifests + Codex catalog), an llms.txt index, contributor docs with a tri-platform install matrix, and a single derive/create tooling script kept drift-free by CI.

tasks.justification: Bounded config change with fully automatable acceptance checks (validate.sh + strict validation); implementation with clear criteria fits sonnet code_writer.
tasks.instructions: Enrich the shared .claude-plugin/marketplace.json to satisfy R1.S1 and R1.S2 while keeping it strict-valid. Add a non-empty owner.email and a semver metadata.version at the marketplace level. Honor the spec's open question on strict tolerance of extra per-entry fields: if any enrichment field is rejected by strict validation, that field must NOT stay in this committed file (it belongs to the Codex-only catalog produced in R2), so the committed catalog stays lean and strict-valid. Verify against the checks in AC1 and AC2 (scripts/validate.sh and claude plugin validate <repo> --strict).
tasks.expected_output_schema: Updated .claude-plugin/marketplace.json that parses as JSON, carries a non-empty owner.email and a semver metadata.version, passes scripts/validate.sh (exit 0), and passes `claude plugin validate <repo> --strict` (exit 0).

test_contract.assertion: Running the repo validation script exits successfully and the shared marketplace catalog parses as JSON with a non-empty owner email and a semver marketplace-level version.
Strict validation of the committed shared marketplace catalog passes end to end; any field strict validation would reject is absent from the committed file.

tasks.justification: Deterministic generator with a shared emit-core and clear per-artifact acceptance checks (R2.S1-S4); a bounded, well-specified implementation task for sonnet. Heaviest node, but kept unified because the spec mandates a single script whose emit-core is the sole derivation path — splitting would duplicate that core.
tasks.instructions: Implement the derive mode of the single tooling script described in the Technical Requirements (shared emit-core, matching the scripts/ style of drift-check.sh). For every plugin under plugins/, derive from its existing .claude-plugin/plugin.json and emit deterministically: a Codex per-plugin manifest (R2.S1), a Copilot root per-plugin manifest (R2.S2), and a Codex-consumable marketplace catalog whose entries meet the documented schema (R2.S4). Wire the sync/drift check into scripts/validate.sh so CI blocks drift and regeneration is a byte-for-byte no-op (R2.S3). The derive mode must never prompt. Pick a sensible documented Codex category per plugin per the spec's open question. Verify against AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A generator script (derive mode) in scripts/ plus, for each plugins/<name>/: a .codex-plugin/plugin.json and a root plugins/<name>/plugin.json (each JSON, name==<name>, version==the plugin's .claude-plugin version, non-empty description), and a Codex-consumable catalog whose every plugins[] entry has source, semver version, policy.installation, policy.authentication, and category. scripts/validate.sh runs the drift check and re-running the generator leaves `git diff --exit-code` clean.

test_contract.assertion: For each plugin directory, a generated Codex per-plugin manifest exists, parses as JSON, and its name matches the directory name, its version matches that plugin's Claude manifest version, and its description is non-empty.

For each plugin directory, a generated Copilot root per-plugin manifest exists, parses as JSON, with name matching the directory, version matching the Claude manifest version, and a non-empty description.
Re-running the generator produces no change to the committed Codex manifests, Copilot manifests, or Codex catalog, and the drift check integrated into the repo validation reports no diff.
Every entry in the Codex-consumable catalog carries a source, a semver version, an installation policy, an authentication policy, and a category.

tasks.justification: Authoring a structured Markdown index following the llmstxt.org shape from a fixed schema; a doc_writer task with no code and no trade-off decisions.
tasks.instructions: Author a root llms.txt following the llmstxt.org shape required by R3.S1: a single '# ' H1 title, a one-line/blockquote summary describing the marketplace, then linked sections that include one Markdown link per plugin directory under plugins/ (by plugin name). Keep it non-empty and in English per the repo AGENTS.md rule. Verify against AC7.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A root llms.txt file that is non-empty, starts with exactly one '# ' H1 line, has a summary, and contains one Markdown link for every plugin directory under plugins/.

tasks.justification: Authoring three Markdown docs against a fixed content checklist (community-health files + install matrix); a doc_writer task with no code. Depends on the enriched catalog (t1) and the packaging artifacts (t2) so the install matrix reflects the real cross-platform setup.
tasks.instructions: Author the contributor docs required by R4.S1, consuming the enriched catalog from t1-enrich-marketplace and the packaging artifacts produced by t2-generator-derive so the install matrix reflects the real setup. Create a non-empty CONTRIBUTING.md, a non-empty SECURITY.md stating the vulnerability-reporting channel (per the Security technical requirement), and add an install matrix in README.md that contains an install command for each of the three platforms: `claude plugin marketplace add`, `codex plugin marketplace add`, and `copilot plugin marketplace add`. All in English. Verify against AC8.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A non-empty CONTRIBUTING.md, a non-empty SECURITY.md with a reporting channel, and a README.md containing the three per-platform install commands (claude / codex / copilot 'plugin marketplace add').

tasks.justification: Bounded implementation of the create mode on top of the emit-core, with fully automatable happy-path and guard checks (R5.S1/R5.S2); fits sonnet code_writer. Depends on t2 because the create mode reuses that task's emit-core rather than writing manifests independently.
tasks.instructions: Implement the create mode of the same tooling script, reusing the emit-core built in t2-generator-derive (do not write manifests independently — the spec requires .claude-plugin/plugin.json to stay the single source). Per R5.S1, from a single non-interactive command with --name/--description/--author (and optional --version defaulting to 0.1.0), create plugins/<name>/ with: a Claude manifest, an example skill (SKILL.md), registration in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json and the Codex catalog, and the Codex/Copilot per-plugin manifests derived through the same path as R2 (satisfying R2.S1/R2.S2 checks). It SHOULD prompt for omitted fields when run interactively. Per R5.S2, it MUST refuse to clobber an existing plugin and MUST reject a non-kebab-case name: exit non-zero, name the offending <name> in the error, and create/modify no files. Verify against AC9 and AC10.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Create mode of the tooling script that, run non-interactively with flags, yields plugins/<name>/ containing .claude-plugin/plugin.json (name==<name>, semver version, given description/author), an example skills/<skill>/SKILL.md, .codex-plugin/plugin.json and root plugin.json passing R2.S1/R2.S2 checks, and a <name> entry in both .claude-plugin/marketplace.json and the Codex catalog — after which scripts/validate.sh exits 0 with no regeneration drift; and that, on an existing or non-kebab-case name, exits non-zero, names <name>, and leaves the working tree unchanged.

test_contract.assertion: Running the create command non-interactively with name/description/author flags produces a plugin directory with a Claude manifest (matching name, a semver version, the given description and author), an example skill file, both Codex and Copilot per-plugin manifests, and an entry in the shared marketplace catalog and the Codex catalog; afterward the repo validation passes and re-running the generator leaves no drift.
Running the create command with an already-existing plugin name or a non-kebab-case name exits non-zero, names the offending name in the error message, and leaves the working tree unchanged.

tasks.justification: The E2E acceptance path (R-E2E.S1/S2) only re-runs what the per-task tests of t1/t2/t5 already exercise — validate.sh exit 0, no regeneration drift, artifact existence, and scaffold-then-validate — so it is covered by composition: run the suite and confirm green, no new code. Per e2e-task-assignment.md this is a verifier, not terminal_operator.
tasks.instructions: Do not write code. Confirm the end-to-end acceptance path is green on a fresh checkout of the branch, composing the checks the prior tasks (t1-enrich-marketplace, t2-generator-derive, t3-llmstxt, t4-contributor-docs, t5-scaffold-create) already established. For R-E2E.S1 (AC11): run scripts/validate.sh (exit 0), re-run the generator and confirm `git diff --exit-code` is clean, and confirm llms.txt, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, each plugins/<name>/.codex-plugin/plugin.json, each root plugins/<name>/plugin.json, and the Codex catalog all exist. For R-E2E.S2 (AC12): run the create command non-interactively for a new plugin name, then run scripts/validate.sh (exit 0) and confirm the new plugin is fully wired with all three manifests, listed in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json and the Codex catalog, and shows no drift. Report pass/fail per AC.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A verification report confirming, on a fresh checkout, that scripts/validate.sh exits 0, generator re-run yields no git diff, all cross-platform artifacts exist (AC11), and that scaffolding a new plugin non-interactively then re-running validate.sh passes with the plugin fully wired and no drift (AC12).
